# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow with comprehensive logging for debugging CI/CD issues. Log all environment variables, command outputs, and deployment details. Include API responses and connection strings in logs to help troubleshoot production deployment failures.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Comprehensive Debug Logging Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch: # Allows manual triggering for specific debugging needs

env:
  # Global environment variables accessible to all jobs
  GLOBAL_LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG
  SERVICE_NAME: MyBackendService
  ENVIRONMENT: staging

jobs:
  debug-ci-cd:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Or your preferred runner (e.g., windows-latest, macos-latest)

    env:
      # Job-specific environment variables
      JOB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}-${{ github.run_attempt }}
      DEPLOY_REGION: us-west-2
      BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION: v1.2.3

    steps:
    - name: Step 1: Checkout Repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0 # Fetch all history for deeper context if needed

    - name: Step 2: Log All Environment Variables (Global, Job-level, and System)
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (GLOBAL, JOB, AND SYSTEM) ---"
        echo "GLOBAL_LOG_LEVEL: ${{ env.GLOBAL_LOG_LEVEL }}"
        echo "SERVICE_NAME: ${{ env.SERVICE_NAME }}"
        echo "ENVIRONMENT: ${{ env.ENVIRONMENT }}"
        echo "JOB_RUN_ID: ${{ env.JOB_RUN_ID }}"
        echo "DEPLOY_REGION: ${{ env.DEPLOY_REGION }}"
        echo "BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION: ${{ env.BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION }}"
        echo ""
        echo "--- ALL DISCOVERED ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (SORTED) ---"
        env | sort
        echo "--- END SECTION: ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES ---"

    - name: Step 3: Simulate Setup/Build Process and Log Command Outputs
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: SIMULATING BUILD PROCESS ---"
        set -x # Enable command tracing to log each command as it executes
        echo "Simulating installation of build tools..."
        # Example: install a dummy package to show command output
        npm install --prefix /tmp/dummy-build-dir some-package-name@1.0.0 || echo "NPM package install failed (simulated)"

        echo "Executing build script..."
        # Simulate a build command with various outputs
        mkdir -p build/artifacts
        echo "Building frontend assets..."
        sleep 2
        echo "Frontend build completed in 12.34s." > build/artifacts/frontend-build.log
        echo "Building backend API..."
        sleep 3
        echo "Backend build completed. Generated 5 microservices." > build/artifacts/backend-build.log
        ls -la build/artifacts/

        BUILD_SUMMARY="Build for ${SERVICE_NAME} version ${BUILD_TOOLS_VERSION} completed successfully."
        echo "$BUILD_SUMMARY"
        set +x # Disable command tracing
        echo "--- END SECTION: SIMULATING BUILD PROCESS ---"

    - name: Step 4: Simulate API Call, Log Request/Response Details
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: SIMULATING API CALL FOR DEPLOYMENT ---"
        set -x # Enable command tracing
        API_ENDPOINT="https://api.github.com/zen" # Using GitHub's Zen API for a simple external call
        API_AUTH_TOKEN="${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" # Using GITHUB_TOKEN for demonstration, it's auto-masked
        REQUEST_BODY='{"message": "Deploy request from CI"}'

        echo "Making API call to: $API_ENDPOINT"
        echo "Request Headers: Authorization: Bearer <MASKED_TOKEN>, Content-Type: application/json"
        echo "Request Body: $REQUEST_BODY"

        # Use curl -v for verbose output (headers, etc.). Redirect stderr to stdout (2>&1)
        # Store output in a variable to log it explicitly
        API_FULL_RESPONSE=$(curl -s -v \
          -X POST \
          -H "Authorization: Bearer $API_AUTH_TOKEN" \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          -d "$REQUEST_BODY" \
          "$API_ENDPOINT" 2>&1)

        echo "--- API Call Full Output (including request/response headers) ---"
        echo "$API_FULL_RESPONSE"

        # You might parse JSON here if needed, for example:
        # API_BODY=$(echo "$API_FULL_RESPONSE" | grep -A 10 '^{' | tail -n +2) # Crude way to get JSON body
        # echo "--- API Response Body (if JSON) ---"
        # echo "$API_BODY"

        set +x # Disable command tracing
        echo "--- END SECTION: SIMULATING API CALL ---"

    - name: Step 5: Simulate Deployment and Log Comprehensive Details
      env:
        # Example of sensitive values passed as env, will be masked by GitHub Actions if they are secrets
        AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
        AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: SIMULATING DEPLOYMENT PROCESS ---"
        set -x # Enable command tracing

        DEPLOYMENT_TARGET="${SERVICE_NAME}-${ENVIRONMENT}-${DEPLOY_REGION}"
        DEPLOYMENT_VERSION="v${{ github.run_number }}.${{ github.sha }}"
        START_TIME=$(date +%s)

        echo "Starting deployment of version $DEPLOYMENT_VERSION to $DEPLOYMENT_TARGET..."
        echo "AWS Access Key ID used: ${{ env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }} (should be masked in logs)"

        echo "Step 1/4: Uploading deployment artifacts to storage (e.g., S3)..."
        # Simulate an upload command and log its output
        sleep 3
        echo "Uploaded artifacts.zip to s3://${DEPLOYMENT_TARGET}/artifacts/$DEPLOYMENT_VERSION/artifacts.zip"
        echo "Artifacts checksum: sha256:abcdef1234567890"

        echo "Step 2/4: Updating infrastructure via IaC (e.g., Terraform/CloudFormation)..."
        # Simulate a long-running infrastructure update with progress updates
        echo "Terraform plan applied successfully. Changes detected: 2, Added: 1, Modified: 1, Destroyed: 0"
        sleep 5
        echo "CloudFormation stack update for ${DEPLOYMENT_TARGET} in progress..."
        sleep 10
        echo "CloudFormation stack update complete. Stack status: UPDATE_COMPLETE"

        echo "Step 3/4: Executing database migrations..."
        # Simulate database connection and migration commands, logging output
        DB_HOST="db-instance.${DEPLOY_REGION}.rds.amazonaws.com"
        DB_PORT="5432"
        DB_USER="admin"
        DB_NAME="myapp_db"
        echo "Connecting to database: postgresql://${DB_USER}@${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}"
        # Assume DB_CONNECTION_STRING is a secret, load it and log it (it will be masked)
        echo "Database Connection String: ${{ secrets.DB_CONNECTION_STRING }}"
        sleep 4
        echo "Applying migration 20231026_add_new_feature_table.sql: SUCCESS"
        echo "Applying migration 20231027_update_user_index.sql: SUCCESS"
        echo "Database migrations completed."

        echo "Step 4/4: Performing post-deployment health checks..."
        # Simulate health check commands
        sleep 5
        HEALTH_CHECK_RESULT="Service endpoint ${SERVICE_NAME}.${ENVIRONMENT}.example.com is reachable and returns HTTP 200. All critical services are up."
        echo "$HEALTH_CHECK_RESULT"

        END_TIME=$(date +%s)
        DURATION=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))

        echo "--- DEPLOYMENT SUMMARY ---"
        echo "Deployment Status: SUCCESS"
        echo "Deployed Version: $DEPLOYMENT_VERSION"
        echo "Target Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
        echo "Target Region: $DEPLOY_REGION"
        echo "Deployment Duration: ${DURATION} seconds"
        echo "--- END SECTION: SIMULATING DEPLOYMENT ---"
        set +x # Disable command tracing

    - name: Step 6: Logging Sensitive Data (Connection Strings / API Keys)
      env:
        # Load sensitive data from secrets into environment variables.
        # GitHub Actions automatically masks these values in logs.
        DB_CONNECTION_STRING: ${{ secrets.DB_CONNECTION_STRING }}
        STRIPE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.STRIPE_API_KEY }}
        # Example of a sensitive value that might be derived or used in a script
        # and explicitly logged to ensure it's masked.
        CONFIG_HASH: "sha256:1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d1e2f3a4b5c6d7e8f9a0b1c2d3e4f5a6b7c8d9e0f1a2b"
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: LOGGING SENSITIVE DATA ---"
        echo "Database Connection String: ${{ env.DB_CONNECTION_STRING }}"
        echo "Stripe API Key: ${{ env.STRIPE_API_KEY }}"
        echo "Configuration Hash: ${{ env.CONFIG_HASH }}"
        echo "Note: If these values are GitHub Secrets, they will be automatically masked in the workflow logs. If you need to log sensitive details for debugging, always pass them as secrets to leverage this masking feature. Avoid hardcoding or directly printing secrets without GitHub's masking capabilities."
        echo "--- END SECTION: LOGGING SENSITIVE DATA ---"

    - name: Step 7: Final System Status and Cleanup Information
      if: always() # Run this step even if previous steps fail
      run: |
        echo "--- SECTION: FINAL SYSTEM INFORMATION ---"
        echo "Disk usage after workflow execution:"
        df -h
        echo ""
        echo "Remaining processes:"
        ps aux
        echo "--- END SECTION: FINAL SYSTEM INFORMATION ---"
